Dachau is an important site for understanding Nazi crimes and World War II history, now the Dachau Memorial (Dachau Memorial), warning of the darkest page in human history.
description.zh: 达豪集中营是纳粹德国最早建立、运行时间最长的集中营之一，于1933年——即希特勒上台当年——建成。在长达12年的运行期间，这里关押过约18.8万名囚犯，约4.15万人死亡。它以医学实验闻名，包括减压实验和疟疾实验等残忍的非人道研究。达豪是理解纳粹罪行和二战历史的重要场所，现为达豪纪念馆（Dachau Memorial），警示着人类历史上最黑暗的一页。

shortDesc.en: Historical courtroom for post-war reckoning of Holocaust masterminds, starting point of modern international law
shortDesc.zh: 战后清算大屠杀元凶的历史法庭，现代国际法的起点

description.en: Between 1945 and 1946, the Nuremberg Trials were held at the Palace of Justice in Nuremberg. In Courtroom 600, the architects of the Holocaust were prosecuted, establishing foundational precedents for modern international human rights and war crimes law. Concepts such as 'crimes against humanity', 'war crimes' and 'crimes of aggression' were first formally defined and used to prosecute national leaders. This building thus became the birthplace of international criminal law, witnessing humanity's first attempt to use law to constrain national power.
description.zh: 1945年至1946年间，纽伦堡审判（Nuremberg Trials）在纽伦堡司法宫举行。这场审判在600号法庭内起诉了大屠杀的策划者，为现代国际人权和战争罪法确立了基础判例。"危害人类罪"、"战争罪"和"侵略罪"等概念首次被正式定义并用于起诉国家领导人。这座建筑因此成为国际刑法的诞生地，见证了人类试图用法律约束国家权力的最初努力。

shortDesc.en: Futurist monuments of Yugoslav anti-fascist resistance, concrete monuments of collective memory
shortDesc.zh: 前南斯拉夫反法西斯抵抗的未来主义纪念，集体记忆的混凝土丰碑

description.en: After World War II, Yugoslavia, led by Josip Broz Tito, pursued a path independent of the Soviet bloc. To unite the diverse Slavic peoples, the government commissioned hundreds of monuments called Spomeniks to commemorate victims of fascism and celebrate the victory of guerrilla struggle. Unlike the highly realistic, politically branded statues favored by Soviet socialist realism, Yugoslav architects - such as Bogdan Bogdanović and Dušan Džamonja - used Brutalism (béton brut, unfinished fair-faced concrete) to create massive, highly abstract and often futuristic structures in remote landscapes. By strictly avoiding specific religious imagery, ethnic markers or overt Communist symbols, these monuments attempted to forge a unified identity around the common narrative of suffering and anti-fascist resistance for a fragile multi-ethnic population. Tragically, as Yugoslavia split into brutal sectarian wars in the 1990s, the unifying message of these concrete monuments was shattered. Today, many of these structures are neglected, becoming eerie, isolated remnants of a failed utopian vision and the enduring complexity of Balkan history.
description.zh: 二战后，由约瑟普·布罗兹·铁托（Josip Broz Tito）领导的南斯拉夫社会主义联邦奉行独立于苏联集团之外的道路。为了团结多元的斯拉夫民族，政府委托建造了数百座被称为Spomeniks的纪念碑，以纪念法西斯主义的受害者并庆祝游击队的解放斗争。 与苏联社会主义现实主义所偏爱的高度写实、带有政治烙印的雕像不同，南斯拉夫建筑师——如博格丹·博格达诺维奇（Bogdan Bogdanović）和杜尚·扎莫尼亚（Dušan Džamonja）——利用粗野主义风格（béton brut，即未修饰的清水混凝土）在偏远的景观中创造了巨大、高度抽象且往往具有未来感的建筑。通过严格避免特定的宗教图像、种族标记或公开的共产党象征，这些纪念碑试图围绕受苦和反法西斯抵抗的共同叙事，为一个脆弱的多民族人口锻造统一的身份认同。 悲剧的是，随着南斯拉夫在1990年代分裂成残酷的宗派战争，这些混凝土巨石的统一信息被粉碎了。如今，其中许多建筑被忽视，成为失败的乌托邦愿景和巴尔干历史持久复杂性中令人毛骨悚然的、孤立的遗迹。

name.zh: 柏林墙与查理检查站 Berlin Wall & Checkpoint Charlie

shortDesc.en: Most vivid representation of the Cold War Iron Curtain, physical symbol of ideological division
shortDesc.zh: 冷战铁幕的最直观展现，意识形态分裂的物理象征

description.en: After the defeat of fascism in 1945, Europe was torn apart by a profound geopolitical rift between the capitalist West (led by the USA) and the communist East (led by the Soviet Union). This Cold War, characterized by proxy conflicts, espionage and the looming threat of nuclear destruction, found its absolute physical manifestation in Berlin. To prevent the massive damage caused by citizens fleeing to the West, the German Democratic Republic (East Germany) built the Berlin Wall in 1961. This barrier literally tore a deadly 'no man's land' through the city, filled with guard towers, anti-vehicle trenches and barbed wire. Checkpoint Charlie was the most famous crossing point between East and West during the Cold War, witnessing the painful separation of ordinary people. The fall of the Berlin Wall on November 9, 1989, marked the end of the Cold War and the acceleration of European unification. After unification in 1990, Germany began demolishing the Berlin Wall, and now only a few ruins remain for posterity.
description.zh: 1945年法西斯主义被击败后，欧洲被资本主义西方（由美国领导）和共产主义东方（由苏联主导）之间的深刻地缘政治裂痕所撕裂。这场以代理冲突、间谍活动和核毁灭隐约威胁为特征冷战，在柏林找到了其绝对的物理表现。 为阻止大量公民逃往西方造成的严重破坏，德意志民主共和国（东德）于1961年修建了柏林墙。这道屏障在城市中生生撕裂出一条致命的"无人区"，布满了守卫塔、反车辆壕沟和铁丝网。查理检查站（Checkpoint Charlie）是当时东西方之间最著名的过境点，见证了冷战时期普通人的痛苦分离。1989年11月9日柏林墙的倒塌，标志着冷战的结束和欧洲统一进程的加速。1990年统一后的德国开始了柏林墙的拆除工作，如今仅存少量遗址供后人凭吊。

shortDesc.en: Physical archive of East German secret police psychological terror, warning of totalitarian control
shortDesc.zh: 东德秘密警察心理恐怖的实体档案，极权主义控制的警示

description.en: The fall of the Berlin Wall revealed one of the darkest secrets of the Communist period - the surveillance system of the East German Ministry for State Security (Ministerium für Staatssicherheit, abbreviated Stasi). The Stasi Museum, located in its former headquarters, documents how this secret police agency meticulously monitored East German citizens' lives through informants, letter opening, wiretapping and psychological control. The museum displays various tools and technologies used by the Stasi to surveil citizens, and how resisters outwitted this massive surveillance system. With approximately 100,000 visitors annually, this is an important site for understanding totalitarian control during the Cold War period.
description.zh: 柏林墙的倒塌揭示了共产主义时期最黑暗的秘密之一——东德国家安全部（Ministerium für Staatssicherheit，简称Stasi）的监控系统。斯塔西博物馆位于其前总部内，记录了这个秘密警察机构如何通过线人、拆信、窃听和心理控制等手段严密监控东德公民生活。博物馆展示了斯塔西用来监视公民的各种工具和技术，以及反抗者们如何智胜这个庞大的监控系统。每年约10万名游客到此了解这段沉重的历史，是理解冷战时期极权主义控制的重要场所。

shortDesc.en: Symbol of post-World War II European reconciliation and democratic unity, landmark of Franco-German friendship
shortDesc.zh: 二战后欧洲和解与民主统一的象征，法德友谊的地标

description.en: As the ultimate geographic symbol of peace and reconciliation after World War II and the Cold War, the European Parliament building located in Strasbourg at the Franco-German border represents the vision of European unification. Its massive 13,000-square-meter glass facade symbolizes the democratic transparency of the European Union, while the unfinished tower signifies that the European project is an ongoing process of continuous construction. Although this building is not the European Parliament's only location (Brussels, Belgium and Luxembourg are also official locations), it is the statutory seat of the European Union treaty and an important symbol of European integration - European countries that emerged from the ruins of World War II have finally found a path of joint peaceful development.
description.zh: 作为二战和冷战后和平与和解的终极地理象征，位于法德交界处斯特拉斯堡的欧洲议会（European Parliament）大厦代表了欧洲走向联合的愿景。其巨大的13000平方米玻璃外立面象征着欧盟的民主透明度，而未完成的塔楼则寓意着欧洲项目是一个不断建设的持续过程。 这座建筑虽然不是欧洲议会唯一的所在地（比利时布鲁塞尔和卢森堡也是官方所在地），却是《欧洲联盟条约》的法定总部所在地，也是欧洲一体化的重要象征——从二战废墟中走来的欧洲国家，终于找到了共同和平发展的道路。
